# Billing & Subscriptions Module LLM Guide

Purpose: recurring plans and subscription state on Stripe for Cloudflare Workers +
D1. Turns the one-off payment module into a monetization stack.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: createPlan, listPlans,
  startSubscription, applyStripeEvent, changePlan, cancelSubscription, recordUsage,
  listSubscriptions, dueForDunning.
- `src/state.ts` maps Stripe statuses to ours and derives access (grantsAccess).
- `src/ports/` defines BillingStore.
- `src/adapters/` contains D1 + memory stores.
- `migrations/0001_billing_subscriptions.sql` owns plans, subscriptions,
  billing_events (idempotency), usage_records.
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

Model:
- Provider module on Stripe. The host normalizes raw Stripe webhooks into
  NormalizedBillingEvent; this module owns subscription state + idempotency.
- Statuses: trialing | active | past_due | unpaid | paused | canceled.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Every Stripe status maps via mapStripeStatus; unknown -> non-access, never active.
- applyStripeEvent is idempotent (event-id ledger); looks up the sub first so an
  early event can retry.
- recordUsage is idempotent on its key.
- Access is derived from status via grantsAccess.

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; no Stripe SDK here. Persistence behind BillingStore.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject createMemoryBillingStore().
- Ask before changing migrations, money mutations, or production deploy.
